epigraph.quote: I sat on a terrace + watched forked lightening strike the mountains in the distance. Sparks seemed to fly where the forks hit the rock.

per_stop.quote: It's amazing looking - titanium + limestone. We walked round the outside in the drizzle.
We had salad + escalope + tuna/sword fish + cold rice pudding - very good.
We drove on + found a campsite on the east side of Mutriku, above the harbour.
We had lunch at Puente de la Reina, which has a lovely old bridge. Simon had pale green beans.
It was in a field of sunflowers.
The cool swimming was marvellous
There are buzzards cirding at the top of diff + storks nests on the churches.
the church, with a lovely doorway into a chapel, + the Judgement portal with Jonah + the Whale.
Eve picked fennel for supper.
Simon weeded one of the yellow arrows on the track by our campsite because pilgrims were missing it.
our best meal in Spain - a glass of gazpacho, cold meats... & liver & chips, then chocolate mousse for pud. Then excellent coffee.
It has been marvellous swimming in the heat & washing my hair in a warm shower afterwards.
We all swam in the bluey water. It was freezing.
Walking around the village after supper was nice. Good views of a mountain valley.
It is so big that an aeroplane flew out of it. During the war the Germans built an aeroplane factory in it.
Last night John beat Eve at scrabble with Clippers over a red - 83 points.
The Cathars Bookshop was closed.
5 very naughty horses terrorising car drivers for bread!!
During the interval we bought juice from a family selling it in the courtyard, apricot juice + almond juice.
We had a lovely relaxed day at this campsite, which is one of the nicest we have ever been to.
The students could communicate so well, inspite of speaking different languages.
We ended up watching our pizzas being made at Ille-sur-Tet.
The washing dried incredibly quickly at Trebe.
Gorgeous shops & I bought a 3m. tablecloth with blue grapes.
Charles bought croissants, pain au chocolat, baguettes & an apple pie to die for. We breakfasted in a lay-by.
The carvings are magnificent — Burgundian influence in heavy folds, very expressive faces & hands — Job's sad face & Judith's red dress.
It's very disappointing that, having wanted to swim for days & being much too hot, it rained in the night.
John knew the hotel was OK because there was a porsche boxster outside
The Bailey Bridge was taken down in 1946 + used as a bridge for a local town over their river for 50 years.
looked at the circular movie of the invasion + remains. Then had an ice cream.
it hailed pine cones on top of the van.
We stopped at Port en Bessin to buy John's birthday cake.
It could see all the way from Cherbourg almost to Pegasus Bridge, but no radar.
the land around is scrubby & barren, so farmers haven't put the soil together to farm again.
We had a delicious supper to mark John's birthday. Iles flottant.
glorious sunshine, tide coming in, but so are purply grey clouds.
We finally reached home at 7. Thomas + Peter were there.
